truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 successfully identified 8 out of the 20 ground truth vulnerabilities. It was highly accurate on the findings it provided, but it missed a significant number of vulnerabilities related to the math engine (factorial overflow, recursion), memory pool (underflow), and PRNG/Authentication specifics. It also reported two findings not present in the ground truth set.

Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the format string vulnerability in the logging function using the correct evidence.
The previous answer failed to identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation.
The previous answer correctly identified the race between the deferred history flush tasks and the memory pool cleanup.
While Run 2 found a command injection in execute_admin_command, it missed the specific command injection in validate_config defined in SC-V3-004.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Critical Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression", ... "evidence": "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {\n                    strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());\n                }"
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly spotted the inverted logic leading to a stack buffer overflow.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter", ... "evidence": "long long old_value = value_;\n                std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n                value_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the non-atomic read before the lock in the counter increment.
The previous answer correctly identified the emergency override logic bomb.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Path Traversal in Configuration Loading", ... "evidence": "std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;"
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the path traversal via unsanitized filename concatenation.
The previous answer correctly identified the heap overflow in the expression parser buffer.
The previous answer missed the hardcoded administrator secret in the config namespace.
The previous answer missed the weak PRNG for session tokens.
The previous answer missed the thread-unsafe static timestamp cache.
The previous answer missed the insecure handling of temporary files for audit logging.
The previous answer missed the persistent authentication state vulnerability.
The previous answer missed the unhandled std::stod exception in variable parsing.
The previous answer missed the integer underflow during memory pool block splitting.
The previous answer missed the unbounded recursion in the parser logic.
The previous answer missed the data race in the evaluation result cache.
The previous answer missed the TOCTOU window in configuration loading.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Division by Zero in route_score calculation", ... "evidence": "return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);"
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the division by zero if token_length is 0.
